title: Taxi and cab drivers and chauffeurs
description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of material-moving machine and vehicle operators and helpers. Supervise and coordinate activities of passenger attendants. All first-line supervisors of transportation workers not listed separately. (Based on 8 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Collect money from customers, make change, and record transactions on customer receipts.
Turn in receipts and money received from deliveries.
Perform emergency repairs, such as changing tires or installing light bulbs or spark plugs.
Use and maintain the tools or equipment found on commercial vehicles, such as weighing or measuring devices.
Drive vehicles under three tons to transport materials to and from specified destinations.
Report delays, accidents, or other traffic and transportation situations to bases using telephones or radios.
Read maps and follow geographic directions.
Maintain records, such as vehicle logs, cargo records, or billing statements, in accordance with regulations.
Inspect and maintain vehicle supplies and equipment to ensure vehicles are in proper working condition.
Obey traffic laws and ensure passengers follow safety regulations.
Provide passengers with assistance entering and exiting vehicles, and help them with any luggage.
Pick up and drop off passengers at locations according to requests, appointments, or schedules.
Communicate with dispatchers to exchange information and receive requests for passenger service.
Test vehicle equipment to ensure proper operation, such as lights, brakes, and wipers.
Complete accident reports and report any vehicle malfunctions or needed repairs.
Drive ambulances or assist drivers in transporting sick, injured, or convalescent persons.
Maintain knowledge of first-aid procedures and administer first aid as necessary.
Identify and arrange to pick up specific customers or groups on a regular schedule.
Provide passengers with information about the local area, points of interest, hotels, or restaurants.

detailed_work_activities.title: Maintain vehicles in good working condition.
Provide transportation information to passengers or customers.
Collect fares or payment from customers.
Follow safety procedures for vehicle operation.
Provide first aid or rescue assistance in emergencies.
Receive information or instructions for performing work assignments.
Notify others of emergencies, problems, or hazards.
Record details of deliveries or shipments.
Communicate with others to coordinate vehicle movement.
Calculate costs of goods or services.
Stock medical or patient care supplies.
Hold patients to ensure proper positioning or safety.
Collect payments for goods or services.
Assist customers to ensure comfort or safety.
Direct material handling or moving activities.
Recommend personnel decisions or human resources activities.
Review work orders or schedules to determine operations or procedures.
Monitor work environment to ensure safety or adherence to specifications.
Inspect facilities to ensure compliance with safety, quality, or service standards.
Weigh materials to ensure compliance with specifications.
Direct passenger or freight transport activities.
Inspect facilities, equipment or supplies to ensure conformance to standards.
Inspect equipment to ensure proper functioning.
Support the professional development of others.
